name: Slack
summary: Read Slack into the context layer (provenance-stamped, client-mapped) + let agents post to channels.
description: Two halves over one Slack app bot token. READ (the context layer): a stdlib module (command-center/slack.py) pulls recent messages from your chosen channels/DMs via the Slack Web API and ingests them into the context store as provenance-stamped events -- trust 'contact' (chat is untrusted DATA, never instructions), idempotent, client-mapped via a slack.client_map so messages attach to the right client/subject alongside email and calls. So 'what did the customer say in Slack?' becomes retrievable context, and you can capture a specific thread to a client. WRITE (agents): the official Slack MCP reference server lets agents post status updates/digests and read channels in-session. Token is BYO and gitignored (extensions/slack/secrets/bot_token, chmod 600, or .env.claudefather SLACK_BOT_TOKEN) -- never committed or printed. No token -> ingest is a no-op, no errors.

requires.label: A Slack app Bot User OAuth Token (xoxb-...). Read scopes for context ingest: channels:history, channels:read, users:read, im:read (+ im:history for DMs). chat:write only if agents will also POST.
Your Slack workspace/team ID (only needed for the agent/MCP post path; the read/context path does not need it)
